name: FM Scanner
description: Live FM radio receiver with RDS display, frequency history, presets, and band scan. Streams audio from a remote site's RTL-SDR dongle (role=scanner) to the browser in real time.

name: Web SDR
description: Browser-based software defined radio with scrolling waterfall display. Tune any frequency within the capture bandwidth. WFM, NFM, and AM demodulation modes. Requires an RTL-SDR dongle on the client machine.

name: DAB Scanner
description: DAB digital radio browser. Scans all Band III channels to discover services, streams selected services as MP3 audio via welle-cli and ffmpeg. Displays DLS (Dynamic Label Segment) scrolling text. Requires welle-cli and ffmpeg on the client machine.

name: Logger
description: Compliance logger for continuous 24/7 recording of monitored streams. Hub mode aggregates recorded streams from all connected sites for remote playback — browse timelines and play audio from any site directly on the hub. Unified full-day seekable timeline, wall-clock timestamps, cross-block mark in/out, automatic segment stitching on export, auto-advance playback. Now-playing metadata integration: polls Planet Radio, Triton, or any JSON API every 30 s; falls back to DLS/RDS. Show-name band above timeline, per-song track band showing exact start/end times, track blocks highlighted in amber. Requires ffmpeg.

name: Meter Wall
description: Full-screen audio meter wall showing live PPM-style level bars for every monitored stream across all connected sites. Peak hold with decay, LUFS-I readout, alert flash, RDS/DLS now-playing, site grouping, configurable grid density, and auto-hiding fullscreen kiosk mode.

name: Zetta Integration
description: RCS Zetta broadcast automation integration. Polls the Zetta SOAP service to show live now-playing data for each station — title, artist, cart number, category. Detects commercial/spot blocks in real time. Provides /api/zetta/status for chain and external integration. Includes WSDL discovery and a raw SOAP debug console. No extra pip packages required.

name: Morning Report
description: Daily morning briefing for broadcast engineers. Auto-generates at 06:00 (configurable) covering the previous calendar day: at-a-glance fault summary, per-chain health table, hourly fault heatmap, auto-detected notable patterns (clustering, above-average faults, recurring issues, clean streaks), and stream quality summary. Hub-only.

name: Signal Path Latency
description: Signal path latency tracking for broadcast chains. Polls all connected sites for comparator delay measurements every 30 seconds, stores 90 days of history in SQLite, and computes rolling baselines. Displays per-comparator SVG sparklines, stable/drifting/alert status badges, and configurable alert thresholds per comparator pair. Hub-only.

name: Push Server
description: Turns this SignalScope hub into a centralised push notification server for iOS and Android. Holds APNs (.p8 key) and FCM (service account JSON) credentials and delivers notifications on behalf of any SignalScope installation that points its Push Server URL here. Includes one-click migration from existing Settings credentials. Hub-only.
